rawDocsComments.body:
Thanks for the review, @adiati98 — I've pushed updates addressing all the comments:

- **Feature-name capitalization** — Applied the `Mautic.FeatureList` fixes where the word refers to the Mautic feature. Where `assets` refers to the built JS/CSS build artifacts (not the Mautic Asset feature), I kept the artifact meaning and cleared Vale accordingly so the text stays accurate.
- **Suggested text** — Applied all three of your suggested-text replacements (Node 24 / automatic-build paragraph, the Parcel + `manifest.json` / `AssetsSubscriber` paragraph, and the git-checkout paragraph).
- **Active voice** — Fixed the `aren't committed` passive usage and changed `like` → `such as`.

Vale now reports no findings for this content. Ready for another look.

Promptless prepared a documentation update related to this change.

Triggered by this PR — the GrapesJS Builder's compiled assets are no longer tracked in git, so anyone working from a git checkout now builds them (auto on `composer install`/`update` and DDEV bootstrap, or manually via `composer gjs-build` / `ddev gjs-build`, Node 24).

Documented the new build workflow in the developer docs (7.2), including the Parcel content-hashing + `manifest.json` that feeds the runtime `AssetsSubscriber`. No user-documentation change is needed since end users install pre-built release packages.

Review: [developer-documentation-new #563](https://github.com/mautic/developer-documentation-new/pull/563)

rawCodeReviews.body:
if im not wrong:

build/package_release.php:73 
leaves plugins/GrapesJsBuilderBundle/node_modules and .parcel-cache in the packaging tree after npm ci && npm run build. 

build/exclude_files.txt:30 
only excludes root node_modules/*, and build/package_release.php:94 also puts the whole plugin dir into the update package, so both release zips can ship the plugin’s dev dependencies/cache. I verified the exclude pattern does not catch nested plugin node_modules; my local build produced ~217M node_modules and ~13M .parcel-cache. Fix by deleting those after the build or adding explicit excludes.

plugins/GrapesJsBuilderBundle/.gitignore:4 
removes dist from source, but only DDEV setup and release packaging build it. A normal source install via composer install/root asset generation does not create manifest.json; then AssetsSubscriber.php:63 returns null and no builder assets load. The PR comment already hints at this. I’d add a root Composer script/post-install hook for the GJS build, or otherwise make mautic:assets:generate cover it.
this looks fine to me

I tested by running `composer install`. It generated the prod files in `plugins/GrapesJsBuilderBundle/Assets/library/js/dist` that aren't tracked by git. I can see the assets are being loaded in the browser from the same location at `
https://mautic-community.ddev.site/plugins/GrapesJsBuilderBundle/Assets/library/js/dist/builder.js?vf405b90b`. And testing various features of the landing page builder. I did not notice any issues. Thanks! 👍

rawCodeComments.body:
Promptless prepared a documentation update related to this change.

Triggered by [api-library PR #349](https://github.com/mautic/api-library/pull/349)

The PHP API library now throws a new `ConnectionException` when a request can't reach Mautic (DNS failure, connection timeout, TLS error), with a message that surfaces the target URL and the underlying transport error. The existing developer docs only documented server-side JSON error responses, so this adds a "Handling connection errors with the API library" subsection with a `try/catch` example.

Review: [docs PR #561](https://github.com/mautic/developer-documentation-new/pull/561)

rawDocsComments.body: @adiati98 Removed the `unlinks?` vocabulary entry from `.github/styles/config/vocabularies/Mautic/accept.txt` as requested. Since Vale then flagged 'unlinks' in the prose, I rephrased that sentence in `docs/rest_api/projects.rst` to use 'remove a Project link' / 'removes every Project link' so it passes Vale without re-adding the vocabulary term. Re-ran Vale on the file with 0 findings. Everything else is unchanged.

Good catches, thank you — and they were connected.

The broken links here and at line 359 were cross-file `:ref:` references to the API v2 page, which don't resolve across files. Rather than convert them to `:doc:`, I removed the v2 content entirely: PR #15569 only ships the v1 Projects API (`standard_entity` / `CommonApiController`) — there's no `/api/v2/projects` route, controller, or test in the PR, so documenting a v2 Projects endpoint here wasn't warranted.

The page now documents only the v1 API the PR actually delivers, and the broken links are gone.

rawDocsComments.body: @promptless-for-oss please rebase to 7.2 branch.

Thanks @adiati98! Rebased the branch onto `7.2` and the PR base is now retargeted to `7.2`. The diff stays minimal — the new `docs/rest_api/company_segments.rst` page plus the toctree entry in `docs/index.rst`. Ready for another look.

Thanks @adiati98! I've addressed the Vale warnings on `docs/rest_api/company_segments.rst`:
- Added `Company Segment` / `Company Segments` to the Mautic accept vocabulary so the headings pass the sentence-case check (same approach already used for other multi-word product names, and consistent with `segments.rst`).
- Reworded the intro to drop "functionality."

Vale now reports 0 errors and 0 warnings on the file. The remaining items are suggestion-level only (e.g. the "published" feature-list hint), which match what the existing `segments.rst` page already ships with, so I left those as-is. Let me know if you'd like those adjusted too.

Thanks for the review, @adiati98! I've addressed all the comments:

- Applied your suggested intro wording.
- On the API Library question: the Mautic API Library doesn't include a Company Segments wrapper yet (no `CompanySegments` class in `lib/Api/`), so I added the note admonition pointing users to the HTTP endpoints rather than the `:xref:` section.
- Changed "published" to "Active or Available" for the `publishedOnly` parameter (Mautic.FeatureList).
- Rewrote the "is referenced" passive-voice instances to active voice, which also clears the flagged "that is" contraction warnings.

Vale now reports 0 errors and 0 warnings on the changed file.

Thanks @adiati98! Added `ABM` to the Mautic Vale accept vocabulary (`.github/styles/config/vocabularies/Mautic/accept.txt`), placed alphabetically. Vale now reports 0 errors and 0 warnings on `docs/rest_api/company_segments.rst`. 🚀

@escopecz Your findings are implemented now
@escopecz Any chance you'll find some time this week to review the changes I've made since your review?

On the `entity_type` column idea (merging into lead_list), I don’t think it would remove much duplication. Most of the duplication here is behavioral (PHP/JS/Twig), not schema-related, so merging the table would not address much of it. It would also introduce real risk:
- Membership can't merge anyway - contacts use lead_lists_leads (FK -> `leads`), companies need their own table (FK -> `companies`). Different relations by nature.
- External plugins could read and write lead_list directly and won't know about a new `entity_type` column. Existing queries that don't filter on it would start pulling company segments into contact context
- ~100 internal references to `lead_list` (campaigns, points, reports, stats, page hits) with hard FKs would all need to become entity_type-aware.
- Columns like `isPreferenceCenter` / `isGlobal` become meaningless for companies, and joins start depending on a runtime flag.

My suggestion would be to keep the tables separate to minimize risk and avoid a BC break for plugins, while focusing on reducing the duplication that was identified.

Are there any specific ideas you have on how we could reduce the loc added? Or any code that currently looks terrible in both segments and company segments and should be changed?

We should go class by class and evaluate if it need to exist alone or if we could reuse the existing one in the contact segments. Perhaps it needs an interface or an abstract class or a trait where we can do 90% of the common stuff.

rawCodeReviews.body:
Overall this code looks really good, nice job 👍 

I've found few issues, have a look.

I was reviewing the company segment rebuild command and comparing it with the optimization I did for contact segments in #15031.

Two things worth addressing:

1. **Missing rebuild ordering** `rebuildDependentSegments` in `app/bundles/LeadBundle/Command/UpdateCompanySegmentsCommand.php`
  
The contact segments command now pre-sorts dependent segments before rebuilding, so that if Segment B depends on Segment A, A is always rebuilt first. The company segment command doesn't have this yet.

2. `company_segments` **filter still uses the old subquery approach**
  
In #15031 I've replaced the `leadlist` ([change](https://github.com/mautic/mautic/pull/15031/changes#diff-fabf6585cb305744a376766784ffd9ae09c10a872978241a8e79dcb8640e5c36L140)) filter with a simple join on the `lead_lists_leads` table. The companies_segments table has the same structure (segment_id, company_id, manually_removed, manually_added), so the same optimization applies. Right now `CompanySegmentMembershipFilterQueryBuilder`
  builds a full recursive subquery for company-to-company-segment filtering, which is the exact pattern we moved away from for performance reasons.

The fix would be to update the company_segments filter in the dictionary to use ForeignValueFilterQueryBuilder the same way:
```
  $this->filters['company_segments'] = [
      'type'          => ForeignValueFilterQueryBuilder::getServiceId(),
      'foreign_table' => 'companies_segments',
      'field'         => 'segment_id',
      'where'         => 'companies_segments.manually_removed = 0',
  ];
```

Both issues are connected: once the filter uses the pre-materialized table, the rebuild ordering becomes critical (same as for contact segments), so rebuildDependentSegments should be added to UpdateCompanySegmentsCommand at the same time.
Just a minor issues left. Overall, it looks very nice 👍
All issues are addressed, the code looks good, and it provides the described segment functionality for companies 👍
Looks good as a User!
I scanned through this massive PR and found some places for improvement. Also the CI must be green.
when I try to deactivate a segment:

I went through the first few files and the issues I reported are repeating in other files. Found some new ones. This is too massive of a PR I could go through as it would take a few days. It seems the whole contact segment code was just copy-pasted. 

Have you consider just adding a new "entity_type" column to the existing lead_list table where we could create segments for companies and for contacts in the same table? And process them slightly different? It could live in the same view in the UI just with a different tag/filter or have its own menu item but basically share the same twig/JS files with the existing segments? This is a big burden on maintenance to add another 11k likes of code.

One conflict on the `7.2` A/B-testing anchor was resolved (both it and the new content preserved), and an unrelated stale change was dropped so the net diff is just the intended `docs/plugin_extensions/emails.rst` update. Vale reports 0 errors and the PR is mergeable against `7.2`.
@promptless-for-oss there are many unrelated, old commits here. Please clean rebase this to 7.2.

@adiati98 Done — thanks for the careful review.

- **Clean rebase to `7.2`:** the branch is now a single clean commit (`831fc1b`) on top of the current `7.2` tip, with all the unrelated/old commits dropped. Net diff is just `docs/plugin_extensions/emails.rst`.
- **Review suggestions applied (all rounds):**
  - Mautic feature-name capitalization (`Pages` → `Landing Pages`, `Page` → `Landing Page`, `form` → `Form`, `contact` → `Contact`) where they refer to Mautic features.
  - Wrapped the `Formatting tokens with getFormattedTokens()` heading, the sort-order intro, and the literal example labels in `.. vale off` / `.. vale on`.
  - Removed first-person pronouns from the token example text and fixed the colon casing.
  - Applied your suggested wording for the `a:` link-token prefix explanation.
  - Applied the suggested sort-order list verbatim.
  - Moved the `.. vale on` statement so both token-sorting sentences fall inside the vale-off block.

Vale now reports **0 errors and 0 warnings** on the changed file, and the PR is mergeable against `7.2`. Ready for another look.

rawDocsComments.body:
Thanks for the review @adiati98 — I've pushed an update addressing all of the comments:

- Rewrote the heading and intro in active voice ("Checking whether Mautic should track a request").
- Applied the Mautic.FeatureList capitalization (Landing Page, Email, Asset) in the affected sentences.
- Converted the bot-detection link to the custom-target style: added a `.. _automatic bot detection:` target and linked with `:ref:`Automatic bot detection <automatic bot detection>``.
- Applied your suggested non-trackable conditions bullet list (kept the `.. vale off`/`.. vale on` directives) and the revised "Automatic bot detection" paragraph verbatim.

Applied your suggestion — `email` → `Email` on the `recipients` parameter description, which clears the Vale `Mautic.FeatureList` warning and matches the casing on the `sent` property. Vale now reports 0 errors / 0 warnings on the file. The update has been pushed to this PR. 👍

The failing **Check links** job looks like a flaky `linkcheck`, not an issue with this PR:

- `make html` (Build docs) passed, so all internal `:ref:` resolve.
- This section adds no external URLs, and `linkcheck` scans the whole doc set — so the broken/slow link is elsewhere, not in this change.
- The **same branch passed earlier** ([run 27923816776](https://github.com/mautic/developer-documentation-new/actions/runs/27923816776)); the failure showed up on a later run with no link-related changes, and other open PRs are flapping on the same step.

Could someone re-run the failed job? It should go green. If it keeps failing, the offending external link is pre-existing/repo-wide (candidate for `linkcheck_ignore`) rather than something from this PR.

Confirmed — I pulled the failed **Check links** logs and your read is correct. Every failure is a `520 Server Error` from external `symfony.com` / `twig.symfony.com` URLs (e.g. `https://symfony.com/doc/5.x/index.html`, `https://twig.symfony.com`), which is a transient upstream outage on Symfony's side. These references live in pre-existing files like `plugins/config`, `plugin_extensions/forms_advanced`, `development-environment/getting_started`, and `plugins/autowiring` — not in this PR's new `POST /emails/ID/example/send` section, which adds no external URLs. The `make html` build also passed, so all internal `:ref:` anchors resolve.

No documentation change is needed here. I attempted to re-run the failed job but don't have the repository admin rights required to trigger a re-run — a maintainer with write/admin access will need to re-run **Check links**, and it should go green once Symfony's docs site recovers.

If `symfony.com` stays down and keeps blocking merges, these are repo-wide external links rather than anything from this PR, so they'd be a candidate for `linkcheck_ignore` (a separate maintenance change, not part of this PR).

rawDocsComments.body:
Thanks for the thorough review, @adiati98! I've pushed updates addressing all of the suggestions:

- Wrapped each section heading (**Customizing Custom Field Groups**, **Get Field Group**, **Field Group properties**, **List Field Groups**, **Create Field Group**, **Edit Field Group**, **Delete Field Group**) in `.. vale off` / `.. vale on`, matching the convention in `rest_api/forms.rst`.
- Reworked the passive "being orphaned" wording into active voice — the note now reads "…renaming a group never orphans the Contact and Company Fields that reference it by alias."
- Moved that `.. note::` about the immutable, auto-generated `alias` to immediately after the page title.
- Aligned the page structure, wording, and internal links with `rest_api/forms.rst` and the adjacent REST API reference pages.

On your question — **is there a `PUT /field-groups/ID/edit` besides `PATCH`?** Yes. The edit endpoint supports both, mirroring the standard Mautic REST API edit pattern (same as Forms and the other entities):

- `PUT /field-groups/ID/edit` — **full replacement**: updates the existing group, or **creates** a new one if the ID doesn't exist. Returns `200 OK` on update, `201 Created` on create.
- `PATCH /field-groups/ID/edit` — **partial update**: updates only the supplied fields, and fails with `404 Not Found` if the ID doesn't exist.

I've documented both verbs explicitly under **Edit Field Group** (HTTP request and Response sections). Vale runs clean on the changed files. Ready for another look 🙏

Thanks @adiati98 — pushed an update addressing all four comments:

1. **`guilabel`**: Removed `:guilabel:` from the Custom Field Groups intro and switched to the bold **Settings** > **Custom Field Groups** path.
2. **Mautic API library**: The library doesn't support the Field Groups endpoints yet — there's no `FieldGroups` client in `mautic/api-library`. So I took the "Otherwise" route: removed the PHP/library code examples and added a bulleted note in the note admonition that library support is pending, matching the pattern in #539.
3. **List response**: Reworded to `* Returns ``200 OK`` when the request successfully retrieves the Field Groups list.` and moved the JSON example under the Response heading, modeled on `rest_api/forms.rst`.
4. **Properties heading**: Switched to the `----------` underline.

More broadly, I restructured the whole page to follow `rest_api/forms.rst` and `point_insights.rst`: every endpoint now reads description → HTTP request → Response (with the `* Returns ...` wording and JSON example) → Properties, with the full property table defined once under **Get Field Group** and referenced elsewhere.

@adiati98 thanks for the review — both suggestions are addressed and pushed to this PR:

- `docs/rest_api/field_groups.rst` — wrapped the **Field Group properties** section heading (and its underline) in `.. vale off` / `.. vale on`, keeping the cross-reference label above it intact.
- `docs/plugin_extensions/contacts.rst` — applied your exact wording for the Custom Field Groups intro paragraph and wrapped it in `.. vale off` / `.. vale on`.

Vale confirms both targeted spots are no longer flagged. Ready for another look.

rawDocsComments.body:
Thanks @adiati98! Addressed both comments in the latest commit:

1. Replaced the parenthetical exception with a `.. note::` admonition and capitalized **Form**.
2. Removed the parentheses (using a dash where needed) and followed the style guide for indentation and blank lines.

The note now reads as you suggested, with the `:ref:` cross-link to *Customizing Forms*. Vale passes clean. Ready for another look.

Thanks @adiati98! Reworked the `features` field per your feedback:

- Fixed the RST indentation so the block now renders on the page.
- Converted the recognized feature values (`email`, `form`, `page`) to an unordered list.
- Replaced the broken links with `:ref:` cross-references to the sections on this page.
- Rewrote the wording in active voice.
- Resolved the `[Google.Colons]` Vale warning (lowercase after the colon).

Pushed to the PR branch — ready for another look.

Thanks @adiati98 — all three suggestions applied in the latest commit, and I dug into why the links weren't rendering.

- **Features list** → bare targets: `:ref:`html/email.html.twig``, `:ref:`html/form.html.twig``, `:ref:`html/page.html.twig``
- **Corresponding-file paragraph** → `:ref:`Twig files``
- **Form style-only Themes note** → `:doc:`/themes/forms``

**Root cause of the broken links:** `docs/conf.py` sets `autosectionlabel_prefix_document = True`, which generates document-prefixed labels (including the literal backticks in headings like `` ``html/email.html.twig`` ``). That's why neither the previous prefixed form nor a plain bare `:ref:` resolved. To make the bare-target syntax you requested actually work, I added explicit reference labels at the four target headings:

```
.. _Twig files:
.. _html/email.html.twig:
.. _html/page.html.twig:
.. _html/form.html.twig:
```

**Verification:** A full Sphinx build confirms all four cross-references now render as real internal links and `:doc:`/themes/forms`` resolves to "Customizing Forms". No new undefined-label or duplicate-label warnings. Vale passes on the edited lines (which sit inside the existing `.. vale off`/`.. vale on` block).

Heads-up: the bare `:ref:` format works only because of those added heading labels — without them, bare targets would still fail under this repo's `autosectionlabel_prefix_document` setting.

Hi @adiati98, I reviewed this against mautic/mautic#16257.

The documentation is mostly aligned with the feature change, but one wording detail should be tightened after the latest review feedback on #16257: the relaxed import requirement now applies only to style-only Form Themes, meaning Themes that declare only the `form` feature and include a Form style override file.

Mixed-feature Themes, for example `features: ["form", "email"]`, should still require the normal template files for the declared features.

Could we update the note to say something like "A Theme that declares only the `form` feature..." and mention that the exception does not apply when the Theme also declares other features such as `email` or `page`?

Once that wording is updated, the docs will match the current implementation.

Thanks @Thecesar85 — good catch. I've updated the docs to scope the relaxed import requirement to **style-only Form Themes**.

The note now states that the exception applies only when a Theme declares **only** the `form` feature (with a Form style override file), so it doesn't require `html/form.html.twig` or `html/message.html.twig`. It also clarifies that the exception does **not** apply to mixed-feature Themes (for example `features: ["form", "email"]` or those that also declare `page`), which still require the normal template files for each declared feature. Verified against the `['form'] === $features` check in mautic/mautic#16257.

Changes are pushed to this PR (`docs/themes/getting_started.rst` and `docs/rest_api/themes.rst`).
/aside @Thecesar85 thanks for the review! Promptless has updated the PR based on your feedback if you can take a look at it. 🙂

Thanks @adiati98 and @promptless-for-oss. I reviewed the updated wording and it now matches the current behavior in mautic/mautic#16257.

The docs now clearly say the exception applies only when `form` is the Theme's sole feature, and that mixed-feature Themes such as `features: ["form", "email"]` or Themes also declaring `page` still require the usual template files.

LGTM from my side.
Thanks for the review, @Thecesar85 — glad the scoped wording matches the current behavior in mautic/mautic#16257. No further changes needed from our side; this is ready whenever you'd like to merge.

Hi @adiati98, I reviewed the latest state after the `7.2` branch update.

The documentation feedback appears fully addressed, all review threads are resolved, Vale passes, and the Read the Docs preview builds successfully.

The remaining `build` failure comes from `make checklinks`: several existing links to `symfony.com` and `twig.symfony.com` returned transient HTTP 520 responses. The links introduced or modified by this PR are not reported as broken, so this does not appear related to the documentation changes.

I tried to rerun the failed job, but GitHub requires repository admin permissions. When convenient, could you please rerun the failed check and refresh the review decision? The current `CHANGES_REQUESTED` state appears to be left over from the earlier review rounds whose threads are now resolved.

Thank you again for your time and help with this documentation update.

The remaining `CHANGES_REQUESTED` state seems to come from the earlier review rounds. When convenient, could you please refresh the review decision? The branch is also marked as behind `7.2`, but there are no current content or CI failures from our side.

rawCodeReviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR updates the CoreBundle theme import validation to allow importing “form style-only” themes—i.e., themes that declare only the `form` feature and ship just a supported form style override template—without requiring the usual `html/message.html.twig` and `html/form.html.twig` files. **Changes:** - Relax theme import required-file validation specifically for `features: ["form"]` themes that include `html/MauticFormBundle/Builder/_style.html.twig` or `html/MauticFormBundle/Builder/style.html.twig`. - Keep existing required-file validation unchanged for mixed-feature themes (e.g., `["form","email"]`). - Add unit tests covering successful install of a form style-only theme and a regression case ensuring mixed-feature themes are not relaxed. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | app/bundles/CoreBundle/Helper/ThemeHelper.php | Adjusts import required-file logic to permit form style-only theme zips when a supported form style override file is present. | | app/bundles/CoreBundle/Tests/Unit/Helper/ThemeHelperTest.php | Adds unit tests for the new relaxed import path and for ensuring mixed-feature themes still fail with missing required files. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

rawCodeReviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR adds Dynamic Web Content–style contact filters to Focus items so that eligible published Focus items can be automatically injected on tracked visits via `mtc.js`, without requiring manual embeds or campaigns. **Changes:** - Adds a nullable `filters` column to `focus` (via migration) and exposes it via serializer groups for Focus API read/write. - Extends the Focus edit UI with a new Filters tab using the existing DWC filters entry type/theme and shared filter JS initialization. - Implements automatic delivery: `mtc.js` conditionally calls a new public `/focus/check` endpoint which returns matching Focus item JS URLs for the tracked contact, with functional tests covering key scenarios. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 17 out of 17 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Translations/en_US/messages.ini | Adds copy for the new Focus filters UI. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Tests/Functional/Entity/FocusRepositoryTest.php | Adds functional coverage for repository gating and filter serialization edge cases. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Tests/Functional/Controller/PublicControllerTest.php | Adds functional coverage for `/focus/check` responses and non-trackable behavior. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Resources/views/FormTheme/Filter/layout.html.twig | Reuses DWC/Lead filter theme blocks for Focus filters rendering. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Resources/views/Focus/form.html.twig | Adds a Filters tab and renders the DWC-style filters UI + templates. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Helper/FocusFilterHelper.php | Adds helper to evaluate stored filters against a contact using DynamicContentHelper. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Form/Type/FocusType.php | Adds the `filters` form field (DWC entry type) and passes filter choice data into the view. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/EventListener/BuildJsSubscriber.php | Conditionally appends `MauticJS.checkFocusItems` to `mtc.js` when needed. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Entity/FocusRepository.php | Adds DBAL queries to fetch/exists-check published Focus items with non-empty filters. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Entity/Focus.php | Adds FiltersEntityTrait metadata and exposes `filters` via API metadata. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Controller/PublicController.php | Adds `/focus/check` endpoint returning matching Focus items for tracked contacts. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Config/config.php | Registers the new public route `mautic_focus_check`. | | plugins/MauticFocusBundle/Assets/js/focus.js | Initializes the shared dynamic filters JS on Focus pages. | | phpstan-baseline.neon | Removes baseline entries made obsolete by improved typing in FiltersEntityTrait. | | app/migrations/Version20260610124929.php | Adds the `focus.filters` LONGTEXT (DC2 array) column. | | app/bundles/DynamicContentBundle/Helper/DynamicContentHelper.php | Makes `filtersMatchContact()` public for reuse by Focus. | | app/bundles/CoreBundle/Entity/FiltersEntityTrait.php | Improves typing, adds Focus serializer groups, and adds a return type for metadata helper. |

rawDocsComments.body:
Thanks for the review, @adiati98!

I've removed the redundant "Available permissions:" label as suggested. The table now follows directly after the introductory paragraph.

Thanks for the review feedback @adiati98! I've addressed all your suggestions:

1. **Professional text example** — Changed from "Jim is super cool!" to "Discussed product demo requirements. Follow-up scheduled for next week."
2. **Total count** — Updated from `1` to `2` to better illustrate list responses
3. **List continuation** — Added `// ...` after the first object to indicate additional items

The changes have been pushed to this PR.

/aside @escopecz, this PR replaces #425 that's closed due to issues with resolving conflicts. All commits and contents from that PR are preserved.

I have a question. Is "Note" introduced in version 7.2 or earlier version? If from earlier, can you advise from which version?

I need this info to know whether we need to backport this PR to other branches and add "Note" and "Notes" to the Mautic feature list in other branches than 7.2.

TIA! ✨

Notes are there from the very beginning (version 1.0.0) I couldn't find any older PR regarding notes than this fix for note API: https://github.com/mautic/mautic/pull/716 from Mautic 1.2.0. So the notes feature itself must have been there before that.

Thanks for the historical context! You're right that Notes have been a core Mautic feature since version 1.0.0.

This documentation PR focuses specifically on the new `lead:notes` permission set introduced in [PR #16067](https://github.com/mautic/mautic/pull/16067), which adds dedicated permissions for Notes management. The Notes API endpoints themselves have existed, but now they have their own permission controls separate from the general Contact permissions.

No changes needed to the documentation based on this feedback - just wanted to acknowledge the helpful context about the feature's history.

Promptless prepared documentation updates related to this change.

Triggered by [PR #16067](https://github.com/mautic/mautic/pull/16067)

**User Documentation:** Documents the new "Notes - User has access to" permission in Roles management and explains the per-note ownership-based edit/delete visibility on the Contact Notes tab.

Review: [Document dedicated Notes permissions](https://github.com/mautic/user-documentation/pull/704)

**Developer Documentation:** Documents the Notes API permission requirements (`lead:notes`) and updates the Contacts API documentation for the List Contact Notes endpoint.

Review: [Document Notes API permissions](https://github.com/mautic/developer-documentation-new/pull/425)

rawCodeReviews.body: I created a user with no notes permissions at all, and it can see all notes also remember to address sonarqube
I’ve left a couple of comments. Please share your thoughts and make the requested changes.
Looks good, approved. Thank you!

Works as expected. Code looks good.

Just a note:
The createdBy filter never existed in API before, but this PR introduces lead:notes:viewown and applies it correctly in the UI only. The switch from lead:leads:* to lead:notes:* in getNotesAction also changes the gate semantics incorrectly (notes permissions checked against contact owner). I'd recommend aligning API with NoteController before merge, or as immediate follow-up.

TY!
Works now!

rawDocsComments.body:
Done! I've addressed all the review feedback:

1. Capitalized "Assets" in headings and text where referring to the Mautic feature
2. Capitalized "UI Components" in the intro text
3. Added `vale off/on` around "Building SCSS during development" heading
4. Replaced "recompiles" with "rebuilds CSS automatically" to resolve the Vale error
5. Clarified Asset Mapper compilation locations (now specifies `var/assets/` and `public/assets/`)
6. Added `vale off/on` around the final paragraph that refers to general assets in the Symfony context
Done! Added `vale off/on` around the "Building Assets for production" heading.

Thanks for the follow-up! This is already addressed in the latest commit (d6638df). The phrase "finalized assets" on line 62 refers to general frontend assets (CSS/JS files), not the Mautic Assets feature, so I wrapped it in `vale off/on` directives (see lines 60-64). You may need to refresh the PR diff view to see the updated file.

Let me know if you have any other concerns!
Fixed! Added vale directives around the "Importmap" term since it's a standard web/Symfony technical term for JavaScript module import maps.
Thanks for the suggestion! I've added "Importmap" to the Vale vocabulary file and removed the vale directives around that term. This is a cleaner approach than using inline vale statements.
@promptless-for-oss please rebase to 7.2 branch
Done! I've rebased the branch onto `7.2` and updated the PR base branch accordingly.

Good catch! All uses of "asset" in this file refer to Symfony's Asset Mapper and generic frontend assets (CSS, JavaScript files), not Mautic's "Asset" feature (downloadable marketing files).

I've updated the document to use lowercase "assets" consistently for the Symfony frontend concept, and renamed the section heading from "Asset architecture" to "Asset Mapper architecture" to make the distinction clearer. The capitalized "Asset Mapper" is retained since that's the proper name of the Symfony component.
Thanks @adiati98! I've applied your suggestion and wrapped the paragraph in `vale off/on` directives. The change has been pushed to the branch.

Hi @adiati98, thanks for the review! I've applied all three suggestions — the affected passages (the production build intro, the build-result description, and the `offline.css` compatibility stylesheet description) are now wrapped in `.. vale off` / `.. vale on` directives as suggested. Vale passes cleanly (0 errors, 0 warnings). The changes are committed and pushed to this PR.

rawCodeComments.body:
@escopecz In this PR, I'm implementing the exact same setup that was done for the marketplace CSS (modern, no grunt)
I really need your help to move this forward by reviewing the code and testing to get it merged -> we can consider this a preparation for upgrading to a newer Bootstrap version that adds a lot of features that will be useful

My plan is to migrate from v3.4.1 to v4.6.x (next step), and keep going until we reach 5.3 (latest)
What makes this process feasible is all Twig templates I've been adding, because replacing something on the template for newer BS version will automatically update EVERYWHERE across the platform

## Pull request overview This PR migrates Mautic’s Core UI styling pipeline from LESS/Grunt to SCSS compiled via SymfonyCasts Sass Bundle and served via Symfony Asset Mapper/Importmap, while keeping Bootstrap at 3.4.1 (via `twbs/bootstrap-sass`) and maintaining legacy compatibility files (e.g., offline page CSS). **Changes:** - Remove Grunt + LESS build tooling and replace the Core entrypoint with SCSS (`app.scss`) compiled via `sass:build` and Asset Mapper. - Introduce Importmap/Asset Mapper configuration and load it from core Twig layouts. - Convert Core + vendor stylesheet sources from LESS to SCSS and adjust asset paths accordingly; add `offline.css` compatibility output. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 89 out of 122 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

Very cool! I'm trying to discuss with myself whether this is a BC break or not but I'm inclined to not being a BC break. Plugins don't use LESS anyway as they'd have to build the CSS from it on installation somehow.

Obviously, this massive change is hard to review. I added some comments to weird changes I noticed.

@andersonjeccel you are right. There is nothing we can do about the warnings from the outdated Bootstrap library.

Even though this is a big change I don't consider it a BC break because:
1. Plugins cannot use LESS/SASS as it needs a build step. So all are using plain CSS.
2. Themes doesn't use SASS at all. Not even core themes. Again, a need for a build step.
3. Nothing changes for the end user. The LESS build was done during development and the CSS was rebuild for each PR that changed LESS. That is improved now. Instead of calling `grunt compile-less` when a SASS file is changed the developer needs to call `bin/console sass:build`. This is how being called during `composer install` so users don't have to think about it.

@andersonjeccel please update also this PR description with all these changes so the documentation bot knows what to update.

I rebuilt the assets (`composer install`) and clicked through the most heavy UI pages like campaign builder, email builder and some random pages. I see no difference from before.

I like that almost 35k lines were deleted while keeping the functionality, making it simpler and more modern 👏  Great job!
Just some minor findings, overall this looks really good. No visible styling issue in the Mautic panel. Nice work 👍
Everything looks good to me 👍
Valid concern by @matbcvo, but not a blocker from my perspective.

I was thinking whether we shouldn't expand the Projects feature this way. If we could define which project can be accessed by which users. The entities (emails, pages, forms) in the Italy Project would be visible only to the users assigned to it. Or something similar. But a concept of teams would make the configuration easier as the admin wouldn't have to select the right users every time.

I tested with various group categories. Here are some example: Channels: After creating 2 users and setting the group permissions (View, Edit same group) this worked perfectly. I tested also with segments, campaigns, contacts. Here is another screenshot how contacts can be accessed by same role holders: In other words, users from the same role can see eachother's contacts.
Tested on Email and Landing Pages and works fine

## Pull request overview This PR introduces “same role” scoped permissions (e.g., `view/edit/delete/publishsamerole`) so users can access content owned by users in the same role, without granting access to content owned by users in other roles. **Changes:** - Extends the core permission system to support `*samerole` permission levels and same-role ownership checks. - Updates multiple bundles’ controllers/menus to recognize the new `viewsamerole` capability and apply role-scoped listing filters. - Adds/updates automated tests and translations to cover/display the new permission options. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 40 out of 40 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

Had a few findings, maybe you could check if it makes sense to implement same group permission checks in the following methods:
- FetchCommonApiController::getEntitiesAction() —> doesn’t use view same role permissions
- ReportApiController::getEntityAction() — doesn’t check for same role
- ReportModel::getReportsWithGraphs() - Dashboard reports of people with same role won’t show
- ContactExportSchedulerModel::prepareData() —> same role contacts can’t be exported


Also, could you make sure to have a look at the copilot findings?

Looks good.

Please take a look at this:

Bulk contact export ignores `viewsamerole` (real gap)
Updates the `contact index` to respect `viewsamerole`, but `batchExportAction` still uses the old `ismine` filter whenever `viewother` is missing.

- `Contact list:` `owner_id IN (all users with the same role)`
- `Bulk export:` `owner_id = current user only`

A user can see contacts owned by teammates in the same role, but export fewer contacts than the list shows.

Related gaps (same pattern, not fixed in PR)
- `SearchSubscriber::onGlobalSearchForContacts()` — contact global search still uses `ismine` only

TY!

One architectural concern worth discussing is that this approach uses roles for two different concepts: the user’s access level and the user’s team/group boundary.

For example, if Italy marketers and Italy managers need different permissions, they cannot both simply use a `Italy` role. The installation may need roles like `Germany Marketer`, `Germany Manager`, `Italy Marketer`, `Italy Manager`, etc.

That can become harder to manage as the number of countries, offices, brands, or teams grows. It also makes cases like users belonging to multiple teams or managers needing cross-team access more difficult to model - this approach is not possible with permissions.

A separate team/group concept could keep these concerns apart: roles define what a user can do, while teams/groups define which records they can access.

Not saying this blocks the PR, but it feels worth discussing so we are clear whether same-role permissions are the right direction here.

Thanks @wieslawgolec for the detailed review! I've updated the documentation to address your feedback:

**Changes made:**
- Updated all code examples to use static methods on `Mautic\CoreBundle\Doctrine\DatabasePlatform` instead of the deprecated `CommonRepository` instance methods
- Replaced `getILikeExpression()` and `getLowerLikeExpression()` with `DatabasePlatform::getCaseInsensitiveLike()` with flag parameters
- Updated platform detection to use `DatabasePlatform::isPostgreSQL()` instead of manual `instanceof` checks
- Added note that `DatabasePlatform` is the single source of truth for platform differences
- Documented key `DatabasePlatform` methods: `getCaseInsensitiveLike()`, `getRegexpExpression()`, `isPostgreSQL()`, `isMySQL()`

Please let me know if you'd like any further adjustments!

The coverage drop comes mostly from the new/changed database abstraction code (schema helpers, repositories, Doctrine subscriber, etc.) that was added for PostgreSQL compatibility.
These parts are already exercised by the existing test suite. All unit + functional tests now run and pass on PostgreSQL (as well as MySQL/MariaDB) in CI.
Anyway, I agree with the report, that some of the PostgreSQL-specific edge cases aren't explicitly covered by dedicated unit tests yet.
I'm not super keen on adding a bunch of PostgreSQL-only tests (it would duplicate a lot and make maintenance heavier).
Instead, I'd rather prefer to extend the existing tests to be platform-aware,  so they automatically adjust their assertions or data setup depending on whether they're running against MySQL, MariaDB or PostgreSQL.
That way we get better coverage for everyone without exploding the test count, which is already not a small number.

@promptless-for-oss can you make a docs PR for this one please. We need one PR for user docs and one PR for the developer docs.

I was quite excited about the [proposal](https://community.mautic.org/processes/roadmap/f/4/proposals/200) as it was suggesting to bend Postgres with polyfills to support Mautic rather than bending Mautic to support Postgres. However, in this PR I can see major Mautic refactoring. Lots of ifs where one query is used for MySql and MariaDB and completely different query is used for Postgres. When you look at the size of this PR it's clear how massive change it is. It'll take weeks just to review this. I'm pretty sure there are tens of bugs hidden in this PR. So we'll need good user base that require Mautic running on Postgres that will help us review and test this PR. Because if it is still just 1% then such a massive change that makes the code even harder to maintain isn't worth it. This is why we've decided to drop support for Posgres in the first place.

I've considered a better database abstraction layer for Mautic for some time. Inserting another abstraction layer for all data-driven interactions is very appealing. Such a mechanism would allow us to use different backend data sources  depending on what's being requested. Have some documents in MongoDB? Go get them through the data layer Mongo datasource. Need to operate on your campaigns from Salesforce? Get them through the data layer's Salesforce datasource. This would prevent us from having to manage the seemingly neverending data sync issues that come hand-in-hand with our current integrations. 

There's plenty of architectural benefits of this approach. By managing the data layer in this way, we could intercept and act on any request for data, modifying or otherwise combining various data sources into a normalized structure. We could take a request like `$this->datalayer->contacts->all()` and pull in local db contacts, remote contacts from an API response, and query the filesystem of an old ERP. We could more easily implement an [event sourcing](https://eventsauce.io/) approach which comes with all sorts of benefits on its own (data rollbacks, replays, insight into what happened for audit purposes). 

That said, such a system could be rather impractical both to implement and in real world usage without major changes that go beyond the scope of what even this PR is trying to accomplish. I'm not opposed to supporting pgsql, but I would like to see it implemented in a particular way if we really want it. I'm open to discussion.

Thanks for the detailed reviews, @escopecz @shinde-rahul @dongilbert. You're right to call out the scattered DB-specific logic and the growth in scope.

The original plan was a small, contained change like the POC: mostly platform tweaks, quoting fixes, and minimal adjustments to keep everything working on both MySQL/MariaDB and PostgreSQL without breaking existing installs. Doctrine 2.x limits cleaner abstraction options, so some conditionals and duplicated query parts crept in as we hit real edge cases in tests and production data. Things like LIKE vs ILIKE (with LOWER), REGEXP differences, date formatting (DATE_FORMAT vs TO_CHAR), GROUP BY strictness, and upsert behavior all needed handling. The PR ended up bigger than intended because covering those reliably took more work.

I won't promise a full cleanup in the next few days, but I will push the specific refactors you suggested: reduce the inline if ($isPostgres) branches where possible, centralize more of the dialect-specific building (following @shinde-rahul's idea of a clearer abstraction point), and clean up the duplicated methods.

All scattered platform checks and all shared code handling the differences between the PostgreSQL and MySQL dialects will be implemented in the DatabasePlatform class (single place to review), which looks like the best place for this purpose. This will eliminate the need for scattered If(platform) checks throughout the core code.

These reviews were actually expected and were exactly the reason this PR was being a draft. To get the working code reviewed early and gather feedback on the approach. The code itself works, but the current implementation is not yet at the production standard. That's why I wanted reviews before finalizing the architecture.

On maintenance: as mentioned before, I will personally handle PostgreSQL-related fixes going forward. I'm already running this fork in production (sending ~10 million emails/month), so any conflict, bug, or incompatibility that surfaces gets fixed on my side. No extra burden on the core team.

So the current plan is to:
- Short term (coming weeks): address the review comments, reduce scattering, improve query handling for the mentioned differences.
- After that: keep the PR focused on compatibility without major refactoring.
- Ongoing: I maintain Postgres fixes; core stays MySQL-first.

@escopecz Update (following @shinde-rahul's idea of a clearer abstraction point):

Consolidated platform-specific logic into the central **Mautic\CoreBundle\Doctrine\DatabasePlatform** class.
Remaining **DatabasePlatform::isPostgreSQL()** checks and why they stay:
1. **app/bundles/InstallBundle/EventListener/DoctrineEventSubscriber.php** → **postgreSqlMySqlCompact()** method - runs only on PostgreSQL during schema generation to create MySQL compatibility functions/operators (e.g. **date_format**, **convert_tz**, **ifnull**, boolean/integer operators). This is intentional and required for fresh installs.
2. **app/bundles/InstallBundle/Helper/SchemaHelper.php** → **installSchema()**, **backupExistingSchema()**, **dropExistingSchema()** - handles PostgreSQL-specific differences during installation like dropping sequences, backing up/renaming sequences, skipping **foreign_key_checks** (which does not exist on PostgreSQL) etc. These paths are required for the installer to work correctly on both platforms.
3. **app/bundles/CoreBundle/Test/MauticMysqlTestCase.php** - several methods use platform checks for transaction/rollback behavior, sequence handling, psql vs mysql commands, and dump/reset SQL generation etc. These are test infrastructure and deliberately platform-aware.
4. Tests:
* app/bundles/LeadBundle/Tests/EventListener/CampaignSubscriberFunctionalTest.php (testRegexOperatorOnDateFieldCondition) - keeps $useCleanupRollback disabled only for MySQL (to avoid "There is no active transaction" exception occur on MySQL - original test creator intention).
* app/bundles/CoreBundle/Tests/Functional/EventListener/MigrationCommandSubscriberTest.php - generated columns assertions on PostgreSQL (they are not immutable in the same way, so the test is marked as partly skipped).
5. 7 legacy migration files - old migrations (not executed on new installs) but must still run successfully when importing/upgrading from very old databases. They contain checks for:
* **foreign_key_checks** (MySQL-only)
* table field types
* charset/collation handling (PostgreSQL uses different syntax/behavior here)

Everywhere else in the codebase, scattered inline **isPostgreSQL()** checks have been removed and replaced with calls to the centralized **DatabasePlatform** helpers.

The remaining checks are isolated to:
* installation schema preparation
* legacy migration compatibility
* test-specific edge cases

Even after refactoring this is a massive change that will make the maintenance way more painful once we merge this. We are having troubles even supporting both MySql and MariaDB as there are more and more differences between the 2 over time.

This seem to me like a high cost low reward type of change. I like PosgreSQL. We started the Public Mautic Marketplace using Supabase. I'd like to use Supabase as Mautic DB as well. But it's still a "nice to have" that won't actually bring much value. Especially to the end user.

Can you please write what value this massive change will bring to Mautic?

> Even after refactoring this is a massive change that will make the maintenance way more painful once we merge this. We are having troubles even supporting both MySql and MariaDB as there are more and more differences between the 2 over time.
> 
> This seem to me like a high cost low reward type of change. I like PosgreSQL. We started the Public Mautic Marketplace using Supabase. I'd like to use Supabase as Mautic DB as well. But it's still a "nice to have" that won't actually bring much value. Especially to the end user.
> 
> Can you please write what value this massive change will bring to Mautic?

Hi @escopecz,
I get the concern. Maintaining DB differences is already tricky with MySQL/MariaDB, and this adds more surface area in the short term.

Pros:
- Opens Mautic to Postgres users (including Supabase, which you mentioned).
- Better standards compliance and stricter query behavior can catch bugs early.
- Community/enterprise interest exists (per forum).

Cons:
- Extra maintenance overhead now, especially with the old Doctrine version forcing heavy DatabasePlatform workarounds.
- Risk of subtle regressions if we miss edge cases.

That said, it's not urgent for production. I can keep it as an experimental/off-main-branch option for now. Most of the platform-specific code will vanish once we move to Doctrine 4.x anyway.
Can park it until the Doctrine upgrade if the cost/benefit doesn't feel right yet.
@wieslawgolec all valid points! Let's focus on Doctrine upgrade then. Good to know there is a simpler way forward.

We've been running this branch (`7.x-postgresql`) against PostgreSQL 16 and hit a reproducible failure on every `doctrine:migrations:migrate` run:

```
SQLSTATE[42P16]: multiple primary keys for table "migrations" are not allowed
```

**Root cause**: `app/config/config.php` applies `PDO::ATTR_STRINGIFY_FETCHES => true` to the connection options unconditionally (it's there as a MySQL/PHP 8.1 workaround). On `pdo_pgsql`, the option also stringifies the booleans returned by DBAL's schema-introspection queries against `pg_catalog` (e.g. `pg_index.indisprimary`/`indisunique`), which breaks DBAL's boolean handling — `introspectTable()` ends up reporting **zero indexes** for every table. doctrine-migrations then thinks the `migrations` table has no primary key and emits `ALTER TABLE migrations ADD PRIMARY KEY (version)` on every run → 42P16.

**Fix that works for us** — make the option conditional on the driver:

```php
'options' => 'pdo_pgsql' === $localConfigParameterBag->get('db_driver')
    ? []
    : [PDO::ATTR_STRINGIFY_FETCHES => true],
```

With that change, introspection is correct and `mautic:install` + all migrations run cleanly on PostgreSQL; we have the branch passing our end-to-end suite (install → login ��� dashboard) with it.

> ```
> ATTR_STRINGIFY_FETCHES
> ```

Hi @thib-d . Thanks for the detailed report and the suggested fix.

I've already removed the unconditional `PDO::ATTR_STRINGIFY_FETCHES` (no longer needed with PHP 8.2+ / MySQL 8.4+). 

Appreciate the help.

rawCodeReviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Reintroduces PostgreSQL support across Mautic 7.x by adding a compatibility layer for DBAL/SQL differences (quoting, LIKE/ILIKE/regex, migrations, installer/config), while keeping MySQL/MariaDB behavior consistent.

**Changes:**
- Updates migrations and schema/index operations to be PostgreSQL-compatible and idempotent across platforms.
- Normalizes query behavior across DBs (identifier quoting, LIKE/ILIKE, regex, DISTINCT semantics, interval/time functions).
- Updates functional/unit tests and installer/config to run deterministically on PostgreSQL as well as MySQL/MariaDB.

### Reviewed changes

**app/bundles/ApiBundle/Controller/FetchCommonApiController.php:503**
* This method now computes `$requestedIds`, `$validIds`, and `$foundIds`, but never actually uses them to populate `$errors` (the previous `$idHelper->hasErrors()` branch is gone). As a result, invalid IDs (non-integers/<=0) and not-found IDs will no longer be reported in the batch error response. Either restore the `BatchIdToEntityHelper` error handling or explicitly call `setBatchError(...)` for invalid keys and for valid-but-missing IDs based on `$foundIds`.
```
        // Extract all requested IDs (including invalid ones for error reporting)
        $requestedIds = $idHelper->getIds(); // associative: original_key => id_value

        // Filter only valid positive integer IDs for the query
        $validIds = [];
        foreach ($requestedIds as $key => $id) {
            if (is_numeric($id) && (int) $id > 0 && (string) (int) $id === (string) $id) {
                $validIds[$key] = (int) $id;
            }
        }

        /** @var AbstractCommonModel<object> $model */
        $model = $model ?: $this->model;

        $entities = [];
        if (!empty($validIds)) {
            $entities = $model->getEntities(
                [
                    'filter' => [
                        'force' => [
                            [
                                'column' => $model->getRepository()->getTableAlias().'.id',
                                'expr'   => 'in',
                                'value'  => array_values($validIds),
                            ],
                        ],
                    ],
                    'ignore_paginator' => true,
                ]
            );
        }

        // It must be associative because the order of entities has changed
        $idHelper->setIsAssociative(true);

        [$entities] = $prepareForSerialization
            ? $this->prepareEntitiesForView($entities)
            : $this->prepareEntityResultsToArray($entities);

        // Set errors for invalid or not-found IDs
        $foundIds = [];
        foreach ($entities as $entity) {
            $foundIds[$entity->getId()] = true;
        }

        // Return the response with matching keys from the request
        if ($returnWithOriginalKeys) {
            if ($entities instanceof \ArrayObject) {
                $entities = $entities->getArrayCopy();
            }

            return $idHelper->orderByOriginalKey($entities);
        }
```
</details>

rawCodeReviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR attempts to fix issue #15030 where numeric value 0 was silently ignored when saving contact fields via REST API. The fix changes the empty value check in `LeadModel::setFieldValues()` from loose comparison (`==`) to strict comparison (`===`), which correctly differentiates between null/empty string and the numeric value 0. **Changes:** - Modified `LeadModel::setFieldValues()` to use strict comparison for empty checks - Updated social cache fallback logic to use strict comparison - Added explanatory comments about the rationale for using strict comparison --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

rawDocsComments.body:
Thanks for the thorough review, @adiati98 — I've applied all of your suggestions and pushed the update to this branch.

Summary of what changed:
- Restructured the page into your subsection layout with the exact heading text/underline styles and `.. vale off` / `.. vale on` guards you suggested.
- **Creating Projects**: `New` → name + optional description, keeping the unique-name error 'A project with this name already exists.'
- **Deleting Projects**: checkbox selection → blue banner → **Delete selected**, and noted that deletion removes references from assigned items but doesn't delete the items themselves.
- **Assigning items** — both flows: the Projects field + **Hit enter to create** (from the item), and the three-step **Add Entities to Project** flow (from the Project).
- **Deleting items from Projects**: added the brief intro plus the three-dots Options menu → **Remove from project** → confirm steps (also corrected the small "form" → "from" typo).
- **Permissions**: bolded **Project permissions** and the separate **Associate with other entities** permission.

On your request to confirm the assignment flow against the codebase (comment #8): I checked the `ProjectBundle` in `mautic/mautic` (7.x) and your instinct was correct. **Add Entities to Project** (`mautic.project.add_entity`) is the primary button that opens the entity-type chooser, so I used your label. The older "Add new to project" text is a secondary per-section button (`mautic.project.add`), which is why your label is the right one for the main flow. I also verified 'Remove from project', the 'Projects' field label, 'Hit enter to create', the duplicate-name error, and the 'Associate with other entities' permission — all exact matches.

Vale passes clean. Ready for another look.

Thanks for the thorough review, @adiati98! I've addressed all of the points:

1. Removed the redundant `Projects overview` subsection header — the intro now flows directly under the main `Projects` title.
2. `To create a Project:` and `To delete one or more Projects:` — bold removed, now plain text.
3. Added the five screenshots under `docs/projects/images/` with snake_case filenames and descriptive alt text (none starting with "Screenshot of"):
   - **`projects_list_view.png`** (intro) — *"Projects list showing the New button and the Projects item in the left menu"*
   - **`create_new_project_form.png`** (Creating Projects) — *"Create new Project screen with Name and Description fields"*
   - **`assign_project_from_email.png`** (Assigning items → From the item) — *"Email edit view with the Projects field used to assign the Email to a Project"*
   - **`add_entities_to_project_button.png`** (between steps 1 and 2 of the From the Project list) — *"Project detail view highlighting the Add Entities to Project button"*
   - **`remove_item_from_project.png`** — this image shows the three-dots Options menu with **Remove from project**, so I placed it under **Deleting items from Projects** (the per-item flow it depicts) rather than under **Deleting Projects**. Happy to move it if you'd prefer. Alt: *"Options menu on a Project item showing the Remove from project action"*

Vale passes clean on the updated page.

Thanks again, @adiati98 — I've applied all of your review suggestions and pushed the update to this branch:

1. **`projects_list_view.png`** — moved to the end of the **Managing Projects** section.
2. **`create_new_project_form.png`** — added a `|` line break with blank lines between the image and the following paragraph about the unique-name check.
3. **New screenshot** — added `delete_selected_projects.png` under `docs/projects/images/` (the Projects list with two Projects selected and the blue 'Delete selected' banner). It's placed in the **Deleting Projects** section after the delete steps, uses snake_case naming, has descriptive alt text ("Projects list with two Projects selected and the Delete selected banner"), and includes the `|` spacing.
4. **`assign_project_from_email.png`** — indented under the **From the item** unordered list item so it aligns with that bullet, with the `|` spacing.
5. **`remove_item_from_project.png`** — added the `|` line break for spacing before the next section.

Vale passes clean on the updated page. Ready for another look.

Thanks @adiati98 — addressed all five in the latest commit:

1. ✅ Added `|` spacing around the assign-from-Email screenshot, indented under the 'From the item' bullet.
2. ✅ Restructured the 'Add Entities to Project' step with the button screenshot indented under it.
3. Investigated `ProjectBundle`. The `project:` search matches the Project name **exactly and case-sensitively** (`ProjectRepositoryTrait::handleProjectFilter` uses `eq('project.name', …)`, no partial match), and it's only registered on the supported list views (Emails, Campaigns, Segments, Forms, Landing Pages, Assets, Text messages, Marketing Messages, Companies, Points, Point Triggers, Stages, Dynamic Content, Focus Items) — **not** the Contacts list, which is the likely cause of the "No Result Found." I've documented the reliable alternative you flagged: clicking the count in the '# Entities' column opens the Project view, which lists all assigned items grouped by type. Both methods are now covered in the "Finding items by Project" section.
4. ✅ Added where to find Roles (Settings > Roles) and cross-linked the existing Roles documentation.
5. ✅ Fixed "entity" → "item" in the remove-from-Project step.

No remaining blockers on my side — Vale passes clean.

Thanks @adiati98 — I've applied all of your review points and pushed the update to this branch:

1. **'item' → 'entity'** throughout the page (and in image alt texts) to follow the UI wording, including the section heading, which is now 'Finding entities by Project'.

2. **Unordered lists** — converted the flagged instruction list to a bulleted list for clarity.

3. **'Finding entities by Project' rewritten as user-facing steps** — removed the technical second paragraph (the `project:` search-command explanation and the case-sensitivity/scope details, which are developer-facing and don't reflect the UI). It now documents the actual workflow:
   - Open a Project's entities by clicking the project name, or by clicking the 'X entities' label in the '# Entities' column — this opens a page listing the entities used in that Project.
   - The search bar (handy when you have many Projects): type the project name (not case sensitive), hit enter, then click the project name or the entities label.

4. **Added your screenshot** at the end of the section showing the Projects list search workflow, with the same `|` spacing and a descriptive alt text.

Vale passes clean on the updated page. Ready for another look.

rawCodeComments.body:
Promptless prepared a documentation update related to this change.

Triggered by [this PR (#16479)](https://github.com/mautic/mautic/pull/16479)

Documented the new list-view filter scope dropdown — the 'Standard' default, how selecting a field composes into the existing `command:value` search syntax (e.g. `firstname:John`), the per-entity field options, the shareable URL behavior, and the inline query-suggestion hint — in the "Searching Mautic" reference (targeting the `7.2` docs branch).

Note: we evaluated the developer-documentation collection too. The new `*SearchScopeProvider` classes are internal wiring with no published interface, service tag, or registration/discovery contract for third parties, so no developer-docs change was warranted at this time.

Review: [Document the list-view filter scope dropdown (docs PR #832)](https://github.com/mautic/user-documentation/pull/832)

Promptless refreshed the documentation for this change now that it's marked ready for review.

Triggered by this PR being marked ready for review.

We reconciled the existing draft against the finalized code and corrected a few details that had drifted (Emails 'Name' option and removal of a non-existent 'Active' option, broader Contacts field list, flag options applying without a typed term, the inline hint now being Contacts-only, and removal of the stale "(...)/more" affordance).

Note: the two screenshots committed to the docs draft are the original PR screenshots and are now slightly stale (truncated field list, "(...)" entry, popover-style hint); the prose no longer relies on those details, but the images may be worth refreshing against a running instance.

We also evaluated the developer-documentation collection: the new `*SearchScopeProvider` classes are internal first-party wiring (no service tag, interface, event, or registration/discovery contract for third parties), so no developer-docs change is warranted.

rawCodeReviews.body: This PR adds a field selector that changes how the search input is interpreted. It makes the existing search commands more discoverable, which is valuable, but its interaction differs from the current filter UI. When filters are applied through the existing popup, their commands remain visible in the search bar: With the new selector, the selected command is represented separately and hidden from the search bar. Should field selection follow the existing filter behavior and keep the resulting command visible, or is this difference intentional? --- I'm not sure how some filters should work with query, like in this example: The results are filtered by Is:Unpublished but "cats" query seems to not have any effect here.

rawCodeComments.body:
Promptless prepared a documentation update related to this change.

Triggered by this PR (update default Contact social media fields).

Updated the Contact field token tables in the user docs to match: removed the Foursquare, Google+, and Skype tokens, renamed Twitter to X (the `{contactfield=twitter}` alias is unchanged), and added TikTok and YouTube tokens. Targets the 7.2 user docs branch.

Review: [docs PR #812](https://github.com/mautic/user-documentation/pull/812)

No developer-documentation change was needed: the developer docs don't document the default field set or the profile-URL parsing logic as an extension surface, and the only mentions of these fields are illustrative REST API example payloads.

rawDocsComments.body:
Thanks for the detailed review, @adiati98! I've pushed a commit addressing all of these:

- **Alt text** — dropped the leading "Screenshot" from both images and reworded them to describe the function, following the UI image guidelines.
- **'email' ordered list** — wrapped the whole list in `.. vale off` / `.. vale on` since "email" there matches the UI label.
- **'published'** — changed to "Active variants".
- **Passive / 'will' / 'contacts'** — rewrote the scheduling summary in active voice, removed "will", and capitalized "Contacts".
- **Cron job cross-reference** — applied your suggested sentence and made the `:ref:` target and the `.. _ab testing for Segment Emails:` anchor label consistent across `emails.rst` and `cron_jobs.rst`.

Vale is clean on the A/B testing content. Let me know if anything else needs adjusting!

Thanks @adiati98 — converted the variant-management bullets in the "Managing variants" section into a numbered (`#.`) list so they read as step-by-step instructions, consistent with the other ordered lists in the section. Pushed to the branch; Vale is clean on the edited lines.

Thanks for the detailed review, @adiati98 �� I've pushed an update addressing all of your suggestions:

- Restructured the "Managing variants" list so adding a variant is a nested ordered (`#.`) sub-list, with edit/remove as separate bullets.
- Applied your wording for the `Select **Schedule sending**` step, the scheduling summary sentence, the **Scheduled for future send** state sentence, and the hidden **Send** button note.
- Updated the headings to "Sending and determining the winner" and "Determine A/B test winner Cron job".
- Removed the separate Cron job section and moved the explanatory text to immediately follow the code block, per your comment.

The A/B testing content remains Vale lint-clean. Ready for another look whenever you have a moment.

Applied your suggestion, @adiati98. The `--id=ID` explanation following the `mautic:email:sendwinner` code block in `docs/configuration/cron_jobs.rst` now reads exactly as suggested:

> The `--id=ID` limits the run to a single parent A/B test Email by ID. If not provided, the command processes all eligible A/B tests.

Changed it from a bullet item to a plain sentence to match your wording, preserved the surrounding RST/indentation, and confirmed the line stays Vale-clean.

Promptless prepared a documentation update related to this change.

Triggered by [mautic/mautic PR #15897 — Add A/B testing for segment emails](https://github.com/mautic/mautic/pull/15897)

This documents the full user-facing A/B testing workflow for Segment Emails: converting a zero-send email via the *Convert to A/B test* card, the conversion dialog settings (*How to pick the winner*, *Wait before picking winner* default 24h, *Test audience size* default 10%), managing variants (at least two required), scheduling sends, winner determination via the `mautic:email:sendwinner` command, and viewing *A/B Test Stats*. It also adds a *Determine A/B test winner cron job* section. This lands in the user documentation (per @escopecz's note above that this belongs in user docs).

Review: [Document A/B testing for Segment Emails — docs PR #811](https://github.com/mautic/user-documentation/pull/811)

rawDocsComments.body: @promptless-for-oss please rebase to 7.2 branch.
Thanks @adiati98! Rebased the branch onto `7.2` and the PR base is now retargeted to `7.2`. The diff stays clean and limited to the intended Company Segments documentation. Ready for another look.

@adiati98 thanks for the review — all suggestions have been addressed and pushed to this branch:

- Applied your suggested wording for the Company Segment field list (**Name** / **Public name** / **Description** / **Active**).
- Applied your suggested cron command options (`--batch-limit`, `--max-companies`, `--segment-id`).
- Applied the dynamic-Segment movement wording, including the `:ref:` to the Company Segment Cron job section.
- Rewrote the passive "be set" phrasing in active voice.
- Verified the publish/unpublish terminology against the `mautic/mautic` source and standardized the doc on the active/inactive and available/unavailable terms accordingly.
- Applied your two Cron-job sentence suggestions (rebuild command intro and the "every 15 minutes, staggered" guidance).

Vale runs clean (0 errors) on the changed RST files. Ready for another look.

Thanks @adiati98! Addressed all the comments in this round:

- Added `ABM` to the Mautic `accept.txt` vocabulary.
- Fixed the `Google.Passive` warning ('are included') by rewriting the **Or** filter bullet in active voice.
- Capitalized `Company` in the Cron job cross-reference (`Mautic.FeatureList`).
- Applied your `vale off`/`vale on` wrapping for the **Modify Company's Segments** reference and the **Editing**/**Cloning**/**Deleting** headings verbatim.
- Also rewrote one more passive sentence ('are referenced') in the delete warning while I was in there.

Vale now runs clean on the file (the only remaining flag is the feature-name H1 title, which is intentionally capitalized and already wrapped). Ready for another look.

@escopecz Any chance you'll find some time this week to review the changes I've made since your review?

On the `entity_type` column idea (merging into lead_list), I don’t think it would remove much duplication. Most of the duplication here is behavioral (PHP/JS/Twig), not schema-related, so merging the table would not address much of it. It would also introduce real risk:
- Membership can't merge anyway - contacts use lead_lists_leads (FK -> `leads`), companies need their own table (FK -> `companies`). Different relations by nature.
- External plugins could read and write lead_list directly and won't know about a new `entity_type` column. Existing queries that don't filter on it would start pulling company segments into contact context
- ~100 internal references to `lead_list` (campaigns, points, reports, stats, page hits) with hard FKs would all need to become entity_type-aware.
- Columns like `isPreferenceCenter` / `isGlobal` become meaningless for companies, and joins start depending on a runtime flag.

My suggestion would be to keep the tables separate to minimize risk and avoid a BC break for plugins, while focusing on reducing the duplication that was identified.

rawCodeReviews.body:
Overall this code looks really good, nice job 👍 

I've found few issues, have a look.

I was reviewing the company segment rebuild command and comparing it with the optimization I did for contact segments in #15031.

Two things worth addressing:

1. **Missing rebuild ordering** `rebuildDependentSegments` in `app/bundles/LeadBundle/Command/UpdateCompanySegmentsCommand.php`
  
The contact segments command now pre-sorts dependent segments before rebuilding, so that if Segment B depends on Segment A, A is always rebuilt first. The company segment command doesn't have this yet.

2. `company_segments` **filter still uses the old subquery approach**
  
In #15031 I've replaced the `leadlist` ([change](https://github.com/mautic/mautic/pull/15031/changes#diff-fabf6585cb305744a376766784ffd9ae09c10a872978241a8e79dcb8640e5c36L140)) filter with a simple join on the `lead_lists_leads` table. The companies_segments table has the same structure (segment_id, company_id, manually_removed, manually_added), so the same optimization applies. Right now `CompanySegmentMembershipFilterQueryBuilder`
  builds a full recursive subquery for company-to-company-segment filtering, which is the exact pattern we moved away from for performance reasons.

The fix would be to update the company_segments filter in the dictionary to use ForeignValueFilterQueryBuilder the same way:
```
  $this->filters['company_segments'] = [
      'type'          => ForeignValueFilterQueryBuilder::getServiceId(),
      'foreign_table' => 'companies_segments',
      'field'         => 'segment_id',
      'where'         => 'companies_segments.manually_removed = 0',
  ];
```

Both issues are connected: once the filter uses the pre-materialized table, the rebuild ordering becomes critical (same as for contact segments), so rebuildDependentSegments should be added to UpdateCompanySegmentsCommand at the same time.
Just a minor issues left. Overall, it looks very nice 👍
All issues are addressed, the code looks good, and it provides the described segment functionality for companies 👍
Looks good as a User!
I scanned through this massive PR and found some places for improvement. Also the CI must be green.
when I try to deactivate a segment:

I went through the first few files and the issues I reported are repeating in other files. Found some new ones. This is too massive of a PR I could go through as it would take a few days. It seems the whole contact segment code was just copy-pasted. 

Have you consider just adding a new "entity_type" column to the existing lead_list table where we could create segments for companies and for contacts in the same table? And process them slightly different? It could live in the same view in the UI just with a different tag/filter or have its own menu item but basically share the same twig/JS files with the existing segments? This is a big burden on maintenance to add another 11k likes of code.

**⚠️ A warning regarding SVG files:**
- This format is not supported by **most email clients**, so it should be excluded from email usage. Reference: https://www.caniemail.com/features/html-svg/

rawDocsComments.body: @promptless-for-oss please rebase to 7.2 branch.

@adiati98 Done — rebased onto `7.2` and retargeted this PR's base branch accordingly.

The `7.2` branch had substantially rewritten the Tokens section (new "Tokenized From addresses" and "Sender resolution hierarchy" subsections), so the branch was rebuilt on top of `7.2` with only the intended content re-applied: the new "Inserting tokens" section now follows the "Sender resolution hierarchy" content in `docs/channels/emails.rst`. The net diff against `7.2` is the intended change. Example token labels were also switched from straight quotes to RST inline code (e.g. ``Contact: First Name``) to satisfy Vale. The PR is now mergeable against `7.2`.

Thanks for the review, @adiati98 — I've pushed updates addressing all four points:

1. **Suggested intro block** — Applied your suggested wording for the intro sentence and wrapped the "Inserting tokens" section body in `.. vale off` / `.. vale on` so the literal UI text isn't flagged.
2. **No parentheses** — Rewrote the Owner bullet to drop the parenthetical: "Fields from the Mautic User assigned to the Contact as its owner."
3. **`DWC` in accept list** — Added `DWC` to `.github/styles/config/vocabularies/Mautic/accept.txt`.
4. **`Mautic.FeatureList` warning** — Resolved; the vocabulary addition plus the `vale off/on` wrapping clear the `category` → `Category` finding on the edited lines.

Vale now reports no findings on the edited section. Ready for another look when you have a moment.
